title: Results
text:
This page presents the main outputs of the baseline LDA experiment.
The objective of the model is to identify recurring themes in French electoral manifestos and make them interpretable through topic summaries and topic distributions.

title: Results
text:
This page presents the main outputs of the baseline LDA experiment.
The objective of the model is to identify recurring themes in French electoral manifestos and make them interpretable through topic summaries and topic distributions.

title: Results
text:
Baseline experiment outputs
The baseline run produces the following artifacts under outputs/baseline_lda/:

data_topics.csv
topics_summary.csv
lda_model.joblib
vectorizer.joblib
run_config.json
topic_labels.json

These artifacts support both interpretation and reuse in inference and API serving.

title: Results
text:
Topic summaries
The file topics_summary.csv contains one row per topic with the most representative words.

Discovered topics







Topic ID
Top words




0
france, social, majorité, françois, mitterrand, uni, président, progrès, socialiste, français


1
politique, gauche, social, droit, emploi, travail, nouveau, pouvoir, voter, droite


2
national, front, vote, français, voter, député, rpr, udf, force, avenir


3
national, général, emploi, france, français, politique, député, union, homme, social


4
français, national, liberté, france, public, front, pays, populaire, enseignement, majorité


5
écologie, vert, écologiste, entente, vie, ecologiste, environnement, politique, ecologie, nouveau


6
socialiste, france, majorité, union, liberté, français, politique, nouveau, voter, parti


7
communiste, gauche, droite, politique, parti, force, français, changement, majorité, voix

title: Results
text:
Interpretation notes
Some thematic patterns are already visible from the top words:

Topic 5 is clearly associated with ecology and environmental discourse.
Topic 7 appears linked to communist and left-right party language.
Topics 2 and 4 seem related to nationalist or right-wing political framing.
Topics 0 and 6 appear closer to socialist or majority-party discourse.
Several topics combine ideological vocabulary with electoral and institutional terms such as vote, député, président, and majorité.

These interpretations are exploratory and should be read as aids to understanding the topic model rather than fixed labels.

title: Results
text:
What the model returns
The experiment produces:

a topic distribution for each manifesto
a most likely topic for each text
a summary of topic keywords
reusable artifacts for prediction on new text

title: Results
text:
Link with the API
The same saved artifacts are reused in the inference layer and exposed through the FastAPI application.
This means the website, the training pipeline, and the deployed API all rely on the same baseline experiment outputs.

title: Results
text:
Next improvement
A stronger presentation layer could add:

a chart of topic prevalence across the corpus
a comparison by political party
a comparison by socio-professional category
one or two concrete manifesto examples with predicted topic distributions

title: API usage
text:
The project includes a FastAPI application that exposes topic inference for new manifesto-like text inputs.
This makes the trained topic model accessible to users who do not need to run the full training pipeline.

title: API usage
text:
The project includes a FastAPI application that exposes topic inference for new manifesto-like text inputs.
This makes the trained topic model accessible to users who do not need to run the full training pipeline.

title: API usage
text:
Run locally
uv run uvicorn app.api:app --reload
Documentation Local Swagger UI: http://127.0.0.1:8000/docs Deployed Swagger UI: https://manifesto-api-sny.lab.sspcloud.fr/docs

title: API usage
text:
Swagger interface
The API is documented through an interactive Swagger UI, available both locally and on the deployed application.

Example request
curl -X POST "http://127.0.0.1:8000/predict_topics" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"text": "Nous voulons défendre la justice sociale."}'
Example response Add here a real JSON response captured from the API, for example:
{
  "processed_text": "vouloir défendre justice social service public",
  "top_topic_id": 4,
  "top_topic_label": "français_national_liberté",
  "top_topic_score": 0.8539606046385947,
  "topic_distribution": [
    0.020875688261488397,
    0.02086718061520562,
    0.020850240568247337,
    0.02087238559487796,
    0.8539606046385947,
    0.020851844617826007,
    0.02085487065904547,
    0.020867185044714447
  ]
}
Typical usage The API can be used to: * test new manifesto excerpts * inspect topic probabilities * integrate the model into other applications * demonstrate the deployed inference layer

title: Dataset
text:
This project analyzes French electoral manifestos using a processed dataset built from the Archelec archive.
The objective is to study how manifesto themes vary across political parties, socio-professional characteristics, and election years.

title: Dataset
text:
This project analyzes French electoral manifestos using a processed dataset built from the Archelec archive.
The objective is to study how manifesto themes vary across political parties, socio-professional characteristics, and election years.

title: Dataset
text:
Data source
The original manifesto material comes from the Archelec archive developed by Sciences Po / CEVIPOF.
In this MLOps version of the project, the training pipeline does not read raw files directly from the repository. Instead, it loads a merged dataset from external object storage.

title: Dataset
text:
Pipeline input
The training pipeline reads a Parquet file provided through the URL_RAW environment variable.
This design keeps the repository lightweight while making the project reproducible on another machine.

title: Dataset
text:
What the dataset contains
The working dataset combines:

manifesto text
election year information
party-related metadata
socio-professional metadata
cleaned text fields used in preprocessing and modeling

These fields allow the project to connect textual themes with political and candidate characteristics.

title: Dataset
text:
Why Parquet is used
The processed dataset is stored in Parquet format because it is efficient for tabular data workflows and convenient for reproducible machine learning pipelines.
It is also easier to reuse in training than a collection of heterogeneous raw source files.

title: Dataset
section: What is not versioned in Git
text:
What is not versioned in Git
To keep the repository clean and portable, the following elements are intentionally not stored in version control:

raw data files
processed outputs
trained model artifacts
logs
secrets and local environment files

title: Dataset
text:
Reproducibility note
The repository includes the code, configuration, and environment definition needed to rerun the pipeline, while the dataset itself is accessed externally through a declared configuration variable.
This separation is important for reproducibility, deployment, and collaboration.

title: Party Influence in French Electoral Manifestos
text:
This project studies whether French electoral manifestos are shaped more strongly by party ideology or by candidates’ socio-professional characteristics.
Using manifesto texts from the Archelec archive, the project applies topic modeling with Latent Dirichlet Allocation (LDA) to identify recurring themes in political discourse across parties, professions, and election years.

title: Party Influence in French Electoral Manifestos
text:
This project studies whether French electoral manifestos are shaped more strongly by party ideology or by candidates’ socio-professional characteristics.
Using manifesto texts from the Archelec archive, the project applies topic modeling with Latent Dirichlet Allocation (LDA) to identify recurring themes in political discourse across parties, professions, and election years.

title: Party Influence in French Electoral Manifestos
section: From research project to MLOps system
text:
From research project to MLOps system
The initial work began as an NLP analysis project. It was then transformed into a reproducible and deployable machine learning system.
The pipeline covers the full workflow:
data -> preprocessing -> feature preparation -> modeling -> outputs -> inference -> API

title: Party Influence in French Electoral Manifestos
text:
Why this project matters
Political manifestos are useful documents for studying how parties and candidates frame public issues.
This project makes that analysis:

reproducible from the command line
modular and testable
reusable through saved artifacts
accessible through an API
deployable in a cloud environment

title: Party Influence in French Electoral Manifestos
text:
Main research question
Are manifesto themes explained more by:

political party affiliation?
or socio-professional background?

title: Party Influence in French Electoral Manifestos
text:
What this website contains
This documentation website presents:

the dataset used in the pipeline
the modeling approach
the deployed API
the system architecture
the main experiment results

title: Party Influence in French Electoral Manifestos
text:
Intended users
This project is designed for:

data science students
political science researchers
instructors

title: Party Influence in French Electoral Manifestos
text:
Project links

GitHub repository
Live API documentation

title: Party Influence in French Electoral Manifestos
text:
Summary
This project shows how an academic NLP workflow can be refactored into a complete MLOps system with reproducibility, modularity, testing, deployment, and public-facing documentation.

title: Method
text:
The project follows a modular NLP and MLOps pipeline:

data loading
text preprocessing
dataset preparation and filtering
topic modeling with LDA
artifact export
inference on new text
API serving with FastAPI

title: Method
text:
The project follows a modular NLP and MLOps pipeline:

data loading
text preprocessing
dataset preparation and filtering
topic modeling with LDA
artifact export
inference on new text
API serving with FastAPI

title: Method
text:
Input data
The training pipeline reads a merged Parquet dataset from external object storage.
The input contains manifesto text together with metadata used for filtering and downstream analysis.

title: Method
text:
Preprocessing
The preprocessing stage prepares manifesto texts for modeling.
Depending on the experiment configuration, the pipeline can run in a baseline mode or in a version with lemmatization.

title: Method
text:
Feature preparation
Before modeling, the dataset is filtered using experiment parameters such as:

number of topics
minimum document length
start year
end year
random seed

These settings are recorded for reproducibility.

title: Method
text:
Modeling choice
The project uses Latent Dirichlet Allocation (LDA) to discover latent themes in manifesto texts.
LDA is appropriate here because the goal is not supervised prediction, but the extraction of interpretable thematic structure from political discourse.

title: Method
text:
Saved artifacts
Each experiment produces reusable outputs, including:

topic assignments by document
topic summaries
trained LDA model
vectorizer
run configuration
human-readable topic labels

title: Method
text:
Inference
Saved artifacts are loaded from an experiment directory and reused to infer topic probabilities on new text inputs.
This makes the project usable beyond training, including through the API layer.

title: Architecture
text:
The project is structured as an end-to-end MLOps pipeline:
data -> preprocessing -> features -> modeling -> outputs -> inference -> API -> deployment

title: Architecture
text:
The project is structured as an end-to-end MLOps pipeline:
data -> preprocessing -> features -> modeling -> outputs -> inference -> API -> deployment

title: Architecture
text:
Main components
The repository is organized into clearly separated modules:

train.py orchestrates the training pipeline
src/data/ handles data loading and output saving
src/preprocessing/ handles text and metadata cleaning
src/features/ prepares filtered datasets
src/models/ contains topic modeling logic
src/inference/ loads saved artifacts and predicts on new text
app/api.py exposes the FastAPI service
deployment/ contains Kubernetes deployment manifests

title: Architecture
text:
Workflow diagram





flowchart LR
    A[External Parquet dataset] --> B[train.py]
    B --> C[Preprocessing]
    C --> D[Feature preparation]
    D --> E[LDA topic model]
    E --> F[Saved artifacts in outputs/]
    F --> G[Inference layer]
    G --> H[FastAPI API]
    H --> I[Docker container]
    I --> J[CI/CD workflows]
    J --> K[Kubernetes deployment]

title: Architecture
text:
Deployment flow
The project includes: * reproducible local execution with uv * automated tests with GitHub Actions * Docker image build and publication * deployment on SSP Cloud * public API documentation through Swagger UI ## Why this architecture matters
This structure separates experimentation from production use. The training pipeline creates reusable artifacts, the inference layer loads them for prediction, and the API exposes the model to external users without requiring them to run the full pipeline themselves.

title: Architecture
text:
Current deployment target
The FastAPI application is deployed online and exposed through a public Swagger interface: https://manifesto-api-sny.lab.sspcloud.fr/docs
